483.reason: [If tokens list is empty (e.g., due to lexical analysis failure or empty input file), this will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException, causing the program to crash instead of gracefully reporting an error.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [If tokens list is empty (e.g., due to lexical analysis failure or empty input file), this will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException, causing the program to crash instead of gracefully reporting an error.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Potential IndexOutOfBoundsException if tokens list is empty

484.reason: Potential NullPointerException if nextTokenRD(1) returns null (e.g., when tokens list has only one token)

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Potential NullPointerException if nextTokenRD(1) returns null (e.g., when tokens list has only one token)

111.reason: The method overwrites any existing error for the same line number. In the test case, the Lexer correctly identifies error 'a' (single &) on line 18, but subsequent cascading parsing errors (like missing ')' or ';') overwrite this with 'j' or 'i'. The fix should check if an error already exists for this line before overwriting, to preserve the root cause error.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The method overwrites any existing error for the same line number. In the test case, the Lexer correctly identifies error 'a' (single &) on line 18, but subsequent cascading parsing errors (like missing ')' or ';') overwrite this with 'j' or 'i'. The fix should check if an error already exists for this line before overwriting, to preserve the root cause error.

759.reason: The parser only checks for the valid '&&' token (Category.AND). It does not detect the invalid single '&' token (which is likely tokenized as an error or ignored/not matched here), so it fails to report error 'a' when a single '&' is encountered in a logical context.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The parser only checks for the valid '&&' token (Category.AND). It does not detect the invalid single '&' token (which is likely tokenized as an error or ignored/not matched here), so it fails to report error 'a' when a single '&' is encountered in a logical context.
